channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Interface complexes unlock ultrahigh-strength martensite
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 20 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02497-7

A martensitic medium-entropy alloy surpasses the long-standing strength–ductility ceiling of conventional ultrahigh-strength steels. The strengthening without sacrificing ductility originates from Mo–B–C interface complexes that stabilize small-angle grain boundaries.
item.dc:title: Interface complexes unlock ultrahigh-strength martensite
item.dc:creator: Min Seok Kim
Eun Soo Park

item.title: Ultralow contact resistance in halide perovskite devices
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 20 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02482-0

A charge transfer between metal oxide and halide perovskites occurring beneath the metal contact surface locally dopes the semiconductor, dramatically reducing contact resistance and enabling efficient low-voltage charge injection.
item.dc:title: Ultralow contact resistance in halide perovskite devices
item.dc:creator: Taoyu Zou
Yong-Young Noh

item.title: Bulk-heterojunction doping in lead halide perovskites for low-resistance metal contacts
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 20 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02485-x

Through van der Waals integration of Ag/Au electrodes onto single-crystal CsPbBr3 thin films and subsequent formation of Ag2O/CsPbBr3 bulk heterojunctions, effective interfacial hole doping is achieved, reducing the contact resistance by two orders of magnitude compared with previously reported contacts of similar composition.
item.dc:title: Bulk-heterojunction doping in lead halide perovskites for low-resistance metal contacts
item.dc:creator: Laiyuan Wang
Boxuan Zhou
Qi Qian
Yihong Ye
Huan Wu
Bangyao Hu
Peiqi Wang
Ao Zhang
Zhong Wan
Dehui Zhang
Kijoon Bang
Shuanghao Zheng
Aamir Hassan Shah
Jingxuan Zhou
Yiliu Wang
Yu Huang
Xiangfeng Duan

item.title: A molten-salt dispersion of lanthanides at the atomic scale
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 19 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02492-y

Persistent synthesis challenges constrain the stabilization of single-atom lanthanide catalysts across many substrates. Using a method based on molten-nitrite chemistry, the atomic dispersion of lanthanide atoms on diverse substrates is achieved and exemplified by Dy1/Pt for acidic hydrogen evolution.
item.dc:title: A molten-salt dispersion of lanthanides at the atomic scale
item.dc:creator: Haoyuan Wang
Chunxiao Liu
Yuan Ji
Hongliang Zeng
Sunpei Hu
Xinyan Zhang
Qisheng Zeng
Jiawei Li
Qinglong Gao
Yao Zhang
Jie Zeng
Xu Li
Tingting Zheng
Qiu Jiang
Chuan Xia

item.title: Multiscale-structured miniaturized 3D force sensors
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 18 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02508-7

Tactile sensing to differentiate normal and shear forces remains a challenge. A force microsensor array based on graphene and a liquid-metal composites decouples normal–shear force sensing, achieving a 200 μm scale and a 0.9 μN force detection limit.
item.dc:title: Multiscale-structured miniaturized 3D force sensors
item.dc:creator: Guolin Yun
Zesheng Chen
Zhuo Chen
Jinrui Chen
Binghan Zhou
Mingfei Xiao
Michael Stevens
Manish Chhowalla
Tawfique Hasan

item.title: Reconstructing into a denser phase
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 13 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02502-z

Tellurium vacancies can drive atomic reconstruction in PdTe2 thin films, converting them into non-layered PdTe. Such a non-stoichiometric phase transition yields large-scale thin-film superconductors and can also create an intermediate heterostructure that enables helicity-sensitive terahertz emission.
item.dc:title: Reconstructing into a denser phase
item.dc:creator: Jaewon Wang
Soon-Yong Kwon
